title: Learning and Research
subtitle: Textbook groups, videos, podcasts, courses, research resources, implementations, and technical foundations.
lede: The source PDF points learners toward the living document, livestreams, Active Inference Insights, textbook groups, readings, code implementations, and domain-specific courses.

sections.heading: How to Start
sections.body: Begin with the living document, the Start page, public livestreams, podcasts, and learning groups. The website gives a stable public map, while the Discord and recurring events provide synchronous participation.

sections.heading: Core Readings
sections.body: The PDF references the 2022 textbook by Thomas Parr, Giovanni Pezzulo, and Karl J. Friston; Maxwell Ramstead's 2023 precis; Jared Tumiel's Spinning Up in Active Inference; Beren Millidge's paper repository; and work on Active Inference ontology and teams.

sections.heading: Courses and Groups
sections.body: Learning surfaces include Fundamentals of Active Inference, Textbook Group, Social Science course, Physics course, RxInfer.jl learning group, Seasonal School, and theoretical neurobiology conversations.

sections.heading: Implementations
sections.body: The source identifies implementations including RxInfer.jl, PyMDP, SPM, and symbolic cognitive robotics. These support practical modeling and experimentation.

cards.title: Textbook Group
cards.text: A collaborative path through Active Inference foundations.

cards.title: Livestreams
cards.text: Public updates, seminars, model streams, guest streams, and educational recordings.

cards.title: Active Inference Insights
cards.text: Conversational podcast material available through public media channels.

cards.title: Research Resources
cards.text: References, repositories, papers, ontologies, and implementation links.

cards.text: A structured learning map for concepts, methods, and applications.

cards.text: RxInfer.jl, PyMDP, SPM, and symbolic cognitive robotics.
